controls.rationale: Protect operational admin surfaces with SSO, source restrictions, and auditability beyond generated passwords.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Argo CD and Grafana are exposed through HTTPS routes behind the shared ALB. Admin credentials are generated by Terraform into AWS Secrets Manager, and Grafana consumes its credential through External Secrets Operator.
controls.gapSummary: SSO/Identity Center, IP allowlists, fine-grained RBAC, and detailed operation auditing are not yet implemented; Argo CD admin credential delivery still depends on the bootstrap/Terraform path.

evidence.summary: Argo CD admin route is exposed through Gateway API.
Grafana admin route is exposed through Gateway API.
Terraform generates Argo CD and Grafana admin credentials into AWS Secrets Manager.
Grafana admin credential is delivered from Secrets Manager through External Secrets Operator.

controls.title: Admin UI authentication needs SSO and source restrictions
controls.objective: Protect operational admin surfaces with SSO, source restrictions, and auditability beyond generated passwords.

controls.rationale: Allow operational users to inspect health, metrics, logs, and alarms without exposing secrets or deployment mutation privileges.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: kube-prometheus-stack, Prometheus ServiceMonitors, Grafana dashboarding, and application dashboards are deployed. Grafana admin credentials are sourced from Secrets Manager through External Secrets Operator. Pod log forwarding is deferred until future AIOps logging design.
controls.gapSummary: Grafana and Argo CD do not yet integrate SSO, IP allowlists, or granular RBAC; secret masking, log centralization, and operation auditability remain limited.

evidence.summary: Monitoring application installs kube-prometheus-stack and Grafana.
Dashboard ConfigMap provides application observability.
Application ServiceMonitor exposes backend metrics to Prometheus.
Grafana admin credential is supplied through External Secrets Operator.

controls.title: Monitoring read access is available but lacks SSO/RBAC hardening
controls.objective: Allow operational users to inspect health, metrics, logs, and alarms without exposing secrets or deployment mutation privileges.

controls.rationale: Support evidence-based incident investigation while preventing unauthorized platform mutation by the assistant.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Runbooks, route smoke checks, Prometheus/Grafana, and future Kira/AIOps queries support investigation of metrics and health; pod log forwarding is deferred.
controls.gapSummary: There is not yet an automated closed loop from alert to incident channel to authorized remediation.

evidence.summary: Runbook index captures operational procedures.
AIOps observability ADR records CloudWatch metrics direction.
Route smoke checks provide operational evidence.
Reports README records that control JSON files are the retained source for future audit/reporting work.

controls.title: AIOps investigation is interactive but not closed-loop automated
controls.objective: Support evidence-based incident investigation while preventing unauthorized platform mutation by the assistant.

controls.rationale: Keep operational credentials out of Git and minimize sensitive values in Terraform state and Kubernetes Secrets.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Argo CD, Grafana, and vintage application database credentials are generated by Terraform into AWS Secrets Manager. External Secrets Operator uses a dedicated IRSA role allowlisted to those secret ARNs and syncs Grafana/vintage Kubernetes Secrets from Secrets Manager.
controls.gapSummary: Secrets still originate in Terraform state and are materialized as Kubernetes Secrets; no dedicated customer-managed KMS key, secret-read audit workflow, or non-Terraform application credential lifecycle exists yet.

evidence.summary: Argo CD and Grafana admin credentials are stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
Vintage application database settings are stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
External Secrets Operator IRSA can read only allowlisted secret ARNs.
Vintage runtime secret is synced from Secrets Manager into Kubernetes.
Grafana admin credential is synced from Secrets Manager into Kubernetes.

controls.title: Operational and application secrets are centralized with remaining state/KMS gaps
controls.objective: Keep operational credentials out of Git and minimize sensitive values in Terraform state and Kubernetes Secrets.

controls.rationale: Rotate, revoke, and redistribute credentials through a controlled workflow without weakening permission boundaries or leaving old credentials valid.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Terraform exposes rotation epoch inputs for Argo CD, Grafana, and vintage database credentials; changing those values creates new Secrets Manager secret versions and updates downstream External Secrets-managed Kubernetes Secrets.
controls.gapSummary: Rotation is manual, not scheduled; revocation, workload restart/rollout, old credential invalidation, and operator approval/audit workflows are not automated end-to-end.

evidence.summary: Admin credential random_password resources use rotation_epoch keepers.
Vintage database password uses a rotation_epoch keeper and stores a new Secrets Manager version.
External Secrets Operator redistributes the vintage secret to Kubernetes.
External Secrets Operator redistributes the Grafana admin secret to Kubernetes.

controls.title: Secret rotation is manually codified but not automated end-to-end
controls.objective: Rotate, revoke, and redistribute credentials through a controlled workflow without weakening permission boundaries or leaving old credentials valid.

controls.rationale: Protect infrastructure state with separated read/write access and reduce sensitive values stored in state.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: GitHub plan and apply roles split S3 state read and write access by state key; the backend uses an external S3 state store.
controls.gapSummary: The state bucket lifecycle and management are outside this repo, and sensitive admin credentials still increase state sensitivity.

evidence.summary: Platform stack uses an S3 backend.
Bootstrap stack uses an S3 backend.
Plan and apply role policies scope Terraform state access.
Workflow writes backend configuration from controlled environment values.

controls.title: Terraform state access is scoped but still sensitive
controls.objective: Protect infrastructure state with separated read/write access and reduce sensitive values stored in state.

controls.rationale: Allow security review of decision history and deployment evidence without granting mutation rights or unnecessary secret access.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Git history, PR discussions, workflow logs, Terraform plan artifacts, and AWS CloudTrail can serve as trace sources.
controls.gapSummary: The repo does not codify centralized audit retention, immutable archival, or reviewer-only audit permissions.

evidence.summary: PR workflow uploads plan artifacts and comments for review.
Deployment workflow produces deployment records.
Destroy workflow records guarded destructive operations.
Control dataset schema supports audit-focused status and evidence reporting.

controls.title: Permission and deployment audit records are available but not immutable
controls.objective: Allow security review of decision history and deployment evidence without granting mutation rights or unnecessary secret access.
